data.description:
Rec 8 of session-logs/2026-06-09-architecture-review.md (risk R9). The store/locking design is ratified as sound — no redesign. Two tunings:
(a) Every write currently does a synchronous best-effort push; for an agent claiming a batch this serializes network latency per write. Add REBAR_PUSH=always|async|off with default ALWAYS (real-time propagation is a first-order requirement); async pushes after returning, still best-effort, fsck still reports PUSH_PENDING.
(b) The mkdir-lock fallback (no-flock platforms, ticket-lib.sh) has unmeasured starvation behavior under many concurrent local agents on macOS. Add a CI stress test (N concurrent writers, assert no lost events, bounded wait).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] REBAR_PUSH honored by all write paths across CLI/library/MCP; default unchanged (always)
- [ ] Convergence semantics unchanged (PUSH_PENDING reporting, pre-push merge retry) under async
- [ ] mkdir-lock stress test in CI; results documented in docs/concurrency.md
data.title: Auto-push policy: REBAR_PUSH=always|async|off (default always) + lock-fallback stress test
